entry_a.title: Hexagonal ports separate domain from adapters
entry_a.content: Domain code depends on ports (Protocol classes), adapters implement them. Swapping an adapter (GitHub API vs. stub) requires no domain changes. PRPort, IssueStorePort, WorkspacePort are the canonical examples.

entry_b.title: Port/adapter architecture
entry_b.content: Our codebase follows hexagonal: abstract ports at the edges, concrete adapters behind them. The orchestrator and phases know about ports only — they accept PRPort etc. via constructor injection. Swapping adapters for tests is the main payoff.

notes: Both describe the same hexagonal port/adapter separation principle.
